topic: A graduate-level lecture on the Martingale Convergence Theorem, covering discrete-time stopping times and the Doob Optional Stopping Theorem with applications to stochastic optimization at the Weierstrass Institute.
An invited seminar on recent advances in Homotopy Type Theory, presenting a univalent foundation model developed jointly with collaborators at the Hausdorff Center for Mathematics.

A research talk presenting new bounds on the chromatic number of distance graphs in Euclidean space, extending the Hadwiger-Nelson problem with constructions from the Erdős Discrete Geometry Workshop.
A lecture on the Atiyah-Singer Index Theorem for elliptic operators on compact manifolds, walking through the heat-kernel proof strategy with concrete examples from spin geometry.
A formal presentation on verified compiler passes in the CompCert project, detailing the translation from Cminor to RTL and describing the Coq proof architecture used at INRIA Saclay.
A talk on the numerical stability of implicit Runge-Kutta methods for stiff ODEs, benchmarking DIRK schemes against BDF methods on chemical kinetics problems from the BZ reaction.
A presentation on lattice-based cryptography in post-quantum TLS handshakes, analyzing the CRYSTALS-Kyber key exchange under side-channel attack models developed at ETH Zürich.
A physics colloquium on the Kibble-Zurek mechanism in quenched superfluid helium-3, presenting defect density measurements from the Lancaster ultra-low temperature laboratory.
A seminar on neutrino mass ordering constraints from the JUNO detector, comparing normal versus inverted hierarchy sensitivities with Monte Carlo simulation outputs from the IHEP collaboration.
A lecture on topological quantum error correction with surface codes, explaining threshold theorems and logical qubit lifetimes measured in the Google Quantum AI Sycamore processor.

An economics seminar on the identification of causal effects in staggered adoption designs, critiquing two-way fixed effects estimators and presenting the Callaway-Sant'Anna diD approach using France's labor reform data.
A seminar on Dynamic Stochastic General Equilibrium models for small open economies, calibrating a New Keynesian framework to Chilean macro data from the Central Bank of Chile.

A lecture on mechanism design for spectrum auctions, comparing the incentives and revenue properties of simultaneous multiple-round auctions versus combinatorial clock auctions used in the FCC 700 MHz band.

domain: investor update for a non-tech business
topic: A quarterly review of the Hapag-Lloyd container shipping fleet, presenting freight rate trajectories, fleet utilization ratios, and EEXI compliance timelines for the Asia-Europe trade lane.

A strategic briefing for the Governing Board of the European Southern Observatory on旗舰 ELT construction milestones, budget variance against the €1.2B envelope, and first-light instrument commissioning schedules.

A briefing for the University of Heidelberg Senate on the Excellence Cluster renewal strategy, detailing planned hires in quantum materials and the collaborative framework with the Max Planck Institute for Medical Research.

A briefing to the Bundestag Digital Committee on post-quantum migration timelines for federal IT infrastructure, surveying NIST PQC standards and migration cost estimates from the BSI national roadmap.

A policy analysis for the OECD Economic Directorate on fiscal consolidation paths for Southern European members, comparing debt sustainability scenarios under baseline and adverse interest-rate shocks.

A grant proposal presentation to the Volkswagen Foundation for the 'Algebraic Structures in Data Science' initiative, outlining a three-year research programme bridging category theory and machine learning at TU Dresden.

A funding proposal to the Sloan Foundation for the Open Logic Project, presenting the case for a free, open-source formal logic textbook covering soundness, completeness, and incompleteness theorems for undergraduate curricula.

A hands-on tutorial on solving elliptic PDEs with the FEniCS finite element platform, discretizing the Poisson equation on the unit disk with Dirichlet boundary conditions for a DEMO fusion reactor divertor model.
A tutorial on Bayesian hierarchical models in Stan, fitting a partial-pooling model to radon measurement data across Minnesota counties as part of the Columbia Statistics Workshop.

A practical lab on automated theorem proving in Lean 4, proving the infinitude of primes and constructing the natural numbers game, delivered at the Formal Methods in Mathematics summer school at CMU.
audience: mathematics students new to proof assistants

domain: public explainer for a technical audience
topic: An explainer on the Riemann Hypothesis for an educated lay audience at the Heidelberg Laureate Forum, covering the zeta function, nontrivial zeros, and the connection to prime counting from Euler to the present.

domain: public explainer for a technical audience
topic: A lecture-demonstration on LIGO's gravitational-wave detections, walking through the interferometer principle, the 2015 GW150914 event, and parameter estimation pipelines for a Science Café audience at DESY Hamburg.

An upper-secondary lesson on proof by mathematical induction, building from the domino analogy to rigorous proofs of sum formulae, including the sum of squares identity, for a Gymnasium class in Bavaria.

An AP Physics C lesson on Gauss's Law for electrostatics, deriving the electric field of an infinite plane and a spherical shell with symmetric charge distributions for a class at Phillips Exeter Academy.

A mini-lecture on the Erlangen Programme of Felix Klein, tracing how the classification of geometries by their symmetry groups reshaped 19th-century mathematics and influenced the later development of gauge theory.

A lecture on the South Sea Bubble and the birth of modern financial regulation, analysing the 1720 crisis, the subsequent Bubble Act, and its parallels to modern securities law at the LSE Economic History seminar.
The 2024 annual report of the Clay Mathematics Institute, summarising the Millennium Prize Problems programme status, workshop proceedings at Oxford, and the successor to the CMI enhancement award.
The 2024 performance report of the DFG Priority Programme on Quantum Geometry, cataloguing funded projects, publication counts, and collaboration networks across 22 German universities.

A Sabermetrics seminar on expected goals (xG) model calibration in the Bundesliga, comparing Poisson regression and gradient-boosted tree approaches using tracking data from the 2023-24 season at the DFB Analytics Summit.

A talk on Markov-chain models for tennis match prediction, fitting transition probabilities to 15 years of ATP tour data and validating against Elo baselines at the MIT Sloan Sports Analytics Conference.
A research talk on persistent homology for shape classification in materials science, applying the Vietoris-Rips complex to nanopore structures from STEM images at the MPI für Eisenforschung.

A seminar on optimal transport and the Wasserstein metric in generative modelling, presenting theoretical convergence guarantees for WGANs with Lipschitz regularization on the Cifar-10 benchmark at the UPC Barcelona seminar.
A quarterly review of the Deutsches Elektronen-Synchrotron (DESY) photon science division, presenting beamtime allocation statistics, user proposal acceptance rates, and PETRA III upgrade milestones.

A proposal from the University of Bonn to the Simons Foundation for a joint 'Geometry and Quantum Field Theory' visitor programme, detailing the planned exchange framework, senior mentor roster, and five-year budget at the Simons Center for Geometry and Physics.

An academic lecture on the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis and its formal investigation through semantic typology, presenting colour-term experiments from the World Colour Survey and Kay-Maffi classification at the MPI for Psycholinguistics.

A lecture on central limit theorems for dependent random variables, covering martingale difference arrays and mixing conditions with applications to MCMC convergence diagnostics from the M"oller Lab at Aarhus University.

A seminar on the Navier-Stokes regularity problem in three dimensions, surveying partial regularity results of Caffarelli-Kohn-Nirenberg and open questions for the Clay Millennium Prize at the Institut Henri Poincaré.

A presentation on formal verification of distributed consensus protocols in Isabelle/HOL, mechanising the safety proof for the Raft algorithm with case-split strategies from the Cambridge Automated Reasoning Group.
